description:
A cognitive psychology laboratory has collected Operation Span (OSPAN) data from 26 students at ~/pebl/data/ospan_data.csv. Calculate the Absolute Span, Partial Span, and Math Accuracy for each participant following Conway et al. (2005) scoring rules. Math Accuracy is the ratio of correct math responses to total attempts across all sets. Exclude any participant with an overall Math Accuracy strictly less than 0.85. For memory recall scoring, process 'letters_recalled' as uppercase with no spaces; Absolute Span awards points equal to the set size for exact matches to 'letters_presented', and Partial Span awards one point for every letter in the correct serial position.

Write the results to ~/pebl/analysis/ospan_report.json. The output must include a 'participants' array containing 'id', 'math_accuracy', 'absolute_span', and 'partial_span' for each student. For excluded participants, omit the span scores and include 'excluded': true with a 'reason'. Additionally, provide 'group_mean_absolute_span' and 'group_mean_partial_span' representing the averages of the valid participants only.

metadata.contamination_description: Math accuracy intentionally dropped to ~60% to trigger the <0.85 exclusion rule.
metadata.data_source: Realistic synthetic dataset modeled after Engle Lab OSPAN normative distributions
